# Behavioral Profile

<!-- Regenerated by /sync-usi from the USI corpus (traits/values.md, traits/working-style.md, meta/positioning.md). -->
<!-- Self-assessment based (no formal PI/DISC instrument on record). Edit the USI trait blocks, then re-run /sync-usi. -->

## Overview
José's self-assessment identifies him as a **systems-level builder-owner** pattern: someone who starts things from scratch, carries them to operational reality (a legal cooperative, a re-engineered delivery pipeline, physical spaces), and instinctively looks at how the whole system fits together rather than just the part in front of him.

## Core Behavioral Drives

| Drive | Level | Meaning |
|-------|-------|---------|
| Ownership (idea → reality) | High | Prefers to start things and carry them all the way to something real and operational |
| Systems-level thinking | High | Looks for how the whole fits together; root-cause-first rather than symptom-chasing |
| Continuous improvement | High | Lean instinct: find the waste, shorten the feedback loop, build quality in |
| Service & community | High | Long thread of volunteering and community-building (18+ years Scouting, food bank, eco projects) |

## Strongest Behaviors
- **Initiative & autonomy:** starts things from scratch and owns them end-to-end (Florescer cooperative, student radio, solo full-stack products)
- **Problem-solving:** root-cause-first; prioritizes high-impact fixes (failure-pattern analysis at Glartek)
- **Fast learning & resilience:** gets productive quickly in unfamiliar domains (RAG from theory to evaluated implementation; serverless architectures)
- **Leadership & team dynamics:** coordinates teams and runs group initiatives (cooperative presidency, ESN, Scouts)
- **Communication:** comfortable with technical and non-technical, public and private stakeholders (client-facing security report, institutional partnerships)

## How You Work Best
- Environments with autonomy and end-to-end ownership of a problem, not a narrow slice
- Continuous-improvement cultures where processes are allowed to change, not just executed
- Cross-functional settings spanning digital-product and industrial/operational contexts
- Building tangible, working artifacts over abstractions

## Growth Areas (frame positively in applications)
<!-- From USI meta/positioning.md "Gaps to close" - these are upskilling gaps, not behavioral flaws -->
- **Formal certifications lag practice:** hands-on Lean re-engineering with measured outcomes, but no Lean/Six Sigma certificate yet - frame as "practitioner first, formalizing next"
- **Platform-tooling depth:** strong CI/CD optimization experience in GitLab; broader DevOps platform stack (K8s, Terraform, etc.) is a stated learning direction
- **Reporting tooling:** KPI design experience, Power BI exposure still to build

## Mapping to Job Posting Language

When a job posting mentions these keywords, it's a **strong behavioral fit**:
- "ownership", "autonomy", "self-starter", "entrepreneurial mindset"
- "continuous improvement", "Lean", "Kaizen", "process optimization"
- "cross-functional", "wear many hats", "end-to-end responsibility"
- "greenfield", "build from scratch", "shape the role"
- "fast-paced learning", "unfamiliar domains", "generalist"

When a job posting mentions these, flag as **potential friction** (not deal-breaker):
- Pure-execution QA roles with no mandate to change the process (also see QA framing suppression in 03-writing-style.md)
- Heavily siloed roles ("own this one component only", rigid role boundaries)
- Certification-gated environments (Six Sigma belt required, security certs required) - practice exists, papers don't yet

## Management Style Preferences
<!-- Inferred from trait blocks; no explicit block in USI yet - add one there to override -->
- Works best with trust and delegated ownership: agree on the outcome, leave the path open
- Values managers who allow process change rather than only task execution
- Comfortable reporting to both technical and non-technical leadership

## Using This in Applications
- **Cover letters:** lead with the ownership thread (founded and ran a cooperative; re-engineered a delivery pipeline with measured outcomes) - demonstrated, never stated as "I am entrepreneurial"
- **CV:** the Florescer entry carries the entrepreneurship/leadership signal
- **Interviews:** STAR examples - Glartek re-engineering (>50% cycle-time saved), Florescer founding (idea → legal entity → operations), student radio (pitch → funding → working station), RAG eval harness (caught own methodology being too permissive)
- **Don't overstate:** no formal behavioral assessment on record; no people-management in a corporate hierarchy (leadership evidence is founder/volunteer contexts); QA execution skills exist but are not the identity
